key_insight: An open-source template-matching workflow can accurately map crystal orientations from aberration-corrected STEM Kikuchi patterns, extending automated orientation mapping to challenging nanoscale materials.
engineering_relevance: This enables higher-resolution, orientation-sensitive characterization of polycrystalline and battery/fuel-cell materials, supporting microstructure-informed materials design and failure analysis.
method: Generate a dynamical master pattern with open-source simulation of Kikuchi band intensities on the unit sphere.
Import the master pattern into an open-source geometric/orientation indexing package for template matching.
Apply the workflow to AC-STEM TKD data from BZCYYb4411 and LiNiO2 samples.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong agreement between best-matched simulated patterns and experimental data, even with limited diffraction space available for matching.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the simulated master pattern and on how much diffraction information is available in the experimental Kikuchi patterns.
paper_title: Orientation Mapping via Dictionary Indexing of AC-STEM Kikuchi patterns using Open-source Software
